disclaimer: Synthetic fictional reports for demonstration only. Not real patient data.

reports.title: Chest CT — incidental pulmonary nodule
reports.rawReport:
INDICATION: 58-year-old with cough; rule out pneumonia.

TECHNIQUE: Non-contrast CT of the chest was performed using standard protocol. Coronal and sagittal reformations were reviewed.

FINDINGS: The lungs are clear without focal consolidation, pleural effusion, or pneumothorax. There is a 6 mm non-calcified nodule in the right upper lobe (series 2, image 41), stable compared with prior CT from 18 months ago. Mediastinal and hilar lymph nodes are not enlarged. The heart size is normal. The visualized upper abdomen is unremarkable.

IMPRESSION:
1. No acute cardiopulmonary process.
2. Stable 6 mm right upper lobe pulmonary nodule; continued annual low-dose CT surveillance per existing protocol is reasonable.
3. No evidence of pneumonia.

reports.title: MRI brain — headache evaluation
reports.rawReport:
INDICATION: 34-year-old with chronic headaches; exclude structural lesion.

TECHNIQUE: MRI of the brain was performed without and with intravenous gadolinium on a 1.5 T system. Sequences include sagittal T1, axial T2, axial FLAIR, diffusion-weighted imaging, and post-contrast T1 in multiple planes.

FINDINGS: No intracranial mass, hemorrhage, or acute infarct. Ventricular size and sulcal pattern are age-appropriate. There is no abnormal parenchymal or leptomeningeal enhancement. The major intracranial flow voids are preserved. The pituitary gland and sella are unremarkable. Paranasal sinuses and mastoid air cells are clear.

IMPRESSION: Normal MRI of the brain. No intracranial mass or acute process identified. Clinical correlation for headache etiology is recommended.

reports.title: Abdominal ultrasound — right upper quadrant pain
reports.rawReport:
INDICATION: 45-year-old with right upper quadrant pain after meals.

TECHNIQUE: Grayscale and Doppler ultrasound of the right upper quadrant was performed with the patient fasting.

FINDINGS: The liver shows homogeneous echotexture without focal lesion. The common bile duct measures 4 mm and is not dilated. The gallbladder contains multiple echogenic calculi with posterior acoustic shadowing; wall thickness is 3 mm without pericholecystic fluid or sonographic Murphy sign. The pancreas and right kidney are partially visualized and appear unremarkable within limits of the study.

IMPRESSION:
1. Cholelithiasis without sonographic evidence of acute cholecystitis.
2. Recommend clinical correlation; surgical consultation may be considered for symptomatic cholelithiasis.
